title: Custom Computer Programming Services

narrative.executive_view: Custom programming has a large, directly exposed delivery workforce and AI coding tools are already common among enterprise developers. The buy-side opportunity is not simply cheaper labor: it is to acquire a client-context-rich firm at a services multiple and convert selected work to repeatable, outcome-linked delivery. That is a demanding transformation, not the base case for a generic staff-augmentation book.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: Code generation, tests, documentation and routine defect work are the most exposed portions of delivery. GitHub's enterprise-developer survey found widespread tool use, while DORA links use and trust to reported productivity. The same evidence leaves review, security, architecture, requirements elicitation, legacy-system interpretation and production accountability in the human workflow; this supports task compression rather than a claim that software teams disappear.
narrative.value_capture: The primary capture risk is commercial. ISG describes application-development outsourcing moving from effort-based contracts toward outcome, experience and subscription structures, and Globant has introduced a metered subscription model for AI-powered engineering. Those are credible routes to retain savings, but they are evidence of a transition led by scaled providers, not proof that a lower-middle-market 541511 firm has escaped hourly or FTE-rate repricing. The selected distribution and moat inputs therefore remain low.
narrative.adoption_timing: Effective use is judged already above the 50% boundary, so the timing input is zero years by rule. This is supported directionally by GitHub's 2023 enterprise survey, the 2025 Stack Overflow survey and DORA's productivity findings, but it is not a measured adoption rate for small US programming shops. Governance, accuracy concerns and review discipline can still make realized savings uneven across client codebases.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The target band is unusually large, and strategic and PE-backed buyers are active across technology services. Accenture reported 23 strategic acquisitions in fiscal 2025, while Alinea tracked 110 IT-services deals in the twelve months through Q1 2025. Alinea's 10.8x average is materially influenced by recurring-revenue MSP and cloud assets, so the selected buy and exit multiples remain below that benchmark for a generic custom-development firm and assume only a modest improvement in repeatability.
narrative.terminal_demand: Terminal demand is PRESSURED, not melting. Enterprises will still need accountable partners for integration, modernization, security, operational continuity and regulated production change, and ISG reports contracts adding governance, remediation and performance accountability. But AI directly lowers the scarce labor input that made outsourced custom programming valuable; if clients keep work in-house or continue to buy hours, the paid external demand and its price can shrink even as delivery becomes more efficient.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: There is no fresh, code-specific owner-age or succession-shortage statistic, so ownership buyability is deliberately low-confidence and no shortage is credited. Sources blend 541511 with application-development outsourcing, managed services, systems integration and global providers; the record marks those boundary mismatches rather than treating them as direct US lower-middle-market observations. Contractor pass-through, bench utilization, client concentration and the share of staff augmentation versus fixed-price work can each change both margins and AI savings capture materially.

sources.title: 2025 Stack Overflow Developer Survey — AI
sources.what_it_supports: Current developer AI-tool/agent use, reported productivity effects, and accuracy/security constraints.

sources.title: GitHub survey: AI's impact on developer experience
sources.what_it_supports: Enterprise-developer survey evidence on AI coding-tool use, perceived benefits, code review and collaboration remaining in the workflow.

sources.title: DORA: Impact of Generative AI in Software Development (v2025.2)
sources.what_it_supports: Reported productivity, the role of trust, and continuing needs for review, testing and responsible use in development.

sources.title: ISG Provider Lens: AI-driven ADM Services 2025
sources.what_it_supports: Application-development outsourcing pricing transition from time-and-materials/fixed price toward outcome, experience and subscription structures, plus broader accountability in contracts.

sources.title: Globant AI Pods announcement
sources.what_it_supports: A scaled IT-services provider's stated move to subscription, token-metered engineering pricing aligned to outcomes rather than effort.

sources.title: Accenture fiscal 2025 financial performance
sources.what_it_supports: Strategic buyer activity: Accenture states it invested about $1.5 billion across 23 strategic acquisitions in fiscal 2025 and reports AI services bookings/revenue.

sources.title: Alinea IT Services Spring 2025 sector reflections
sources.what_it_supports: IT-services M&A volume, average EBITDA multiple and recurring-revenue/MSP premium caveat for service-business multiple selection.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: AI coding use and productivity evidence supports substantial task compression in code, tests and documentation, while trust, review and client-accountability limits keep each role fraction below full automation.
ai_replaceable_share.plausible_range: 0.26-0.43 of total wage bill after role weighting
caveats: The sources survey developers or development organizations, not occupations within US NAICS 541511.
The supplied role mix is four-digit 5415 and understates the code-delivery concentration of a pure custom-programming shop.
This input measures technical task replaceability only; it does not take credit for adoption speed, client demand or pricing.

role_judgments.rationale: Generation, refactoring, tests and documentation are exposed, but architecture, review, debugging in client context and production accountability remain.
Tier-one questions and routine troubleshooting are highly amenable to assistants, while escalations and client empathy persist.
Status, allocation and reporting compress, but delivery leadership, client escalation and risk ownership remain human-accountable.
Documentation and process analysis are assisted, while eliciting ambiguous requirements and organizational navigation remain.
Proposal drafting and account research compress, but relationship selling and commercial judgment remain.
Internal operations and recruiting administration automate, but executive client relationships and accountability do not.
This mixed computer-occupations category includes technical implementation work exposed to AI, tempered by specialized administration and security work.
Scheduling, status and documentation compress, while scope trade-offs, stakeholder alignment and escalation remain.
Research, analysis drafts and process mapping are assisted, but client diagnosis and change management remain.
Test generation and routine QA are directly exposed, with test strategy, release risk and acceptance judgment persisting.
The unlisted wage share likely mixes additional billable developers with administration; a mid-range fraction avoids treating it as either all code or all back office.

pi_dist.rationale: Effort-based pricing is transitioning toward outcome, experience and subscription structures, but the latter are emerging models and the typical small custom-programming engagement can still pass efficiency to the client through fewer hours or lower FTE need.
caveats: ISG covers broader application-development outsourcing and Globant is a scaled provider, not a US lower-middle-market 541511 sample.
No fresh source measures the T&M, staff-augmentation or fixed-price mix for the target EBITDA band.

pi_moat.rationale: Broader AI-driven ADM contracts can add governance, remediation and operational accountability, which creates some client-specific retention; a custom-development project's code, documentation and people can nevertheless be transferred, so this is a modest moat rather than a durable license-like one.
caveats: The source includes managed services and global systems integrators, which often have stickier contracts than a project-oriented 541511 firm.
This is a capture input only and does not use human accountability to claim a terminal-demand floor.

t50_years.rationale: The selected current effective adoption estimate is already above 50%, so the frozen rule sets t50 to zero rather than forecasting additional years.
t50_years.plausible_range: 0 years if effective adoption is at least 50%; otherwise 1-3 years
caveats: Tool use and self-reported productivity are not identical to effective adoption across all billable tasks.
The evidence is not a small-firm, NAICS-specific longitudinal adoption measure.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: A deliberately discounted effective-use estimate remains above 50% given GitHub's high enterprise tool-use figures, DORA's reported productivity effects and current Stack Overflow evidence, while recognizing that not every user applies AI across meaningful client delivery.
caveats: GitHub's survey was 500 US developers at companies with 1,000+ employees and predates the current tool generation.
Stack Overflow and DORA do not isolate US lower-middle-market custom-programming firms.

historical_analogs.value: AI coding tools extend earlier developer automation by moving from assistance into generation, testing and documentation; the current change is broad but still constrained by trust, review and deployment accountability.
historical_analogs.rationale: Developer surveys and DORA evidence show workflow efficiency layered into ongoing engineering rather than a fully autonomous delivery process.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Incremental workflow compression to material role redesign
caveats: This is qualitative context only and is not used to set adoption timing beyond the separate adoption inputs.

selection.rationale: No fresh, code-specific owner-age statistic was found for US 541511, so a conservative nonzero estimate is used without claiming a sector-54 succession pattern.
caveats: This is not a measured owner-age rate and should not be read as evidence of a succession wave.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: No documentation of a 541511-specific succession shortage was found in the fresh research pass, so no shortage credit is taken.
succession_shortage_documented.plausible_range: true/false; selected false absent direct documentation
caveats: Absence of a located source is not evidence that no individual firm faces succession risk.

active_consolidators.rationale: A bounded count of plausible strategic and PE-backed technology-services buyers is used: Accenture reports sustained acquisition activity, and the IT-services sources describe strategic and PE-backed consolidation. It is intentionally not a claim that eight buyers are bidding for every small 541511 target.
caveats: The evidence covers broad IT services, including MSPs, cloud and systems integration rather than a deal tally for 541511.
This factor is used only for buyability and does not support adoption, capture or terminal demand.

buy_mult.rationale: Alinea's 10.8x average includes premiums for strategic assets and cloud capability and is heavily influenced by MSP demand for recurring revenue. A generic custom-programming/staff-augmentation operator therefore receives a below-benchmark 7.5x entry estimate.
caveats: This is a UK IT-services benchmark, not disclosed US lower-middle-market 541511 transactions.
Contractor pass-through and owner compensation normalization can materially change an apparent EBITDA multiple.

exit_mult.rationale: A 9.5x exit remains below Alinea's broad 10.8x IT-services average and assumes the buyer has converted some delivery into repeatable, outcome-linked contracts; ISG identifies that commercial direction but does not demonstrate it for every small firm.
caveats: No direct evidence supports multiple expansion for a generic 541511 firm; the selected exit is conditional on contract and revenue-quality improvement.
The source mix includes managed services and cloud assets with more recurring revenue than custom programming.

pricing_structure.value: Historically effort-based time-and-materials, fixed-price and staff-augmentation engagements are transitioning selectively toward outcome-, experience- and subscription-linked structures.
pricing_structure.rationale: ISG explicitly describes conventional T&M/fixed-price contracting giving way to outcome and experience agreements, while Globant's AI Pods give a concrete subscription example.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: Predominantly effort-based to meaningfully outcome/subscription-linked depending on client and provider
caveats: No source quantifies the current pricing-model mix for US $1-10M EBITDA 541511 operators.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: The task-replaceability estimate and current adoption are distinct: role fractions describe technically compressible work, while adoption is discounted for effective use. A zero t50 follows only from the selected adoption level, not from the large target pool or consolidation evidence.

terminal_value.justification: Integration, security, governance and production accountability sustain paid work, but AI attacks the scarce coding labor and effort-priced revenue base. Outcome pricing may preserve selected operators' economics; it does not establish durable demand for generic external custom programming.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No broad IT-services or global-provider source is presented as a direct NAICS 541511 small-firm measurement. Each such scope mismatch is stated in the relevant caveats and market-boundary disclosure.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Pricing-model deflation is modeled in pi_dist: fewer billed hours and smaller staff-augmentation needs pass AI productivity to clients unless a firm actually shifts to outcome, subscription or reusable-product economics. It is not counted again as task replaceability.

dataset_mismatch.description: Labor share combines 2024 wages with 2022 receipts, while the supplied occupational mix is 4-digit 5415 rather than six-digit custom programming. The receipts baseline predates broad coding-assistant use.
sensitivity: A more code-heavy six-digit role mix could raise the task-replaceability-weighted value input.
A post-AI receipts and labor series could show a different labor-share denominator and margin structure.

subsegments: Custom software development and maintenance to client specifications
Staff augmentation and effort-based programming engagements
Application development outsourcing and managed application services
Systems integration, MSP/facility management and recurring cloud services
market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Custom software development and staff-augmentation delivery, excluding MSP/facility-management economics where possible.
market_boundary.selection_basis: The NAICS title anchors the analysis to writing or modifying client-specific software. ISG, Alinea and public-provider evidence are used only as broader application-development/IT-services proxies and are discounted where managed-service recurrence, systems-integration scope or large-provider scale differs.

top_uncertain_inputs.plausible_range: 0 years if the discounted effective-adoption estimate is at least 50%; 1-3 years if tool use has not translated into meaningful billable delivery.
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: Crossing below 50% would replace the zero-year timing score with a materially lower score.
Capture changes linearly with distribution; widespread T&M/staff-augmentation repricing would keep it near the low end.
The modest exit uplift is conditional on revenue-quality improvement; a flat services multiple would reduce the multiple factor materially.
No direct owner-age source exists in this record, so buyability can move meaningfully with actual founder demographics.

reviewer_note: Treat the commercial-model transition as the underwriting question. A target with recurring, accountable application work and authority to price outcomes may retain AI savings; an interchangeable FTE/staff-augmentation book may instead experience revenue deflation and should not receive the selected exit case without evidence.
